content.full_text:
CLEAN OCEANS

CLEAN FOOD
CLEAN AIR

YES on 128-
BIG GREEN

STEERING COMMITTEE:
California League of Conservation
Voters
Campaign California
National Toxics Campaign
Natural Resources Defense
Council
Pesticide Watch
Sierra Club California
Assemblyman Tom Hayden, Chair
Attorney General John Van de Kamp

927 Broadway, Suite 301-A
Santa Monica, CA 90401
(213) 395-1990

926 J Street, Suite 300
Sacramento, CA 95814
(916) 447-8950

1095 Market Street, Suite 705
San Francisco, CA 94103
(415) 863-3050

YES on 128 - BIG GREEN
Sierra Club, T. Hayden, J. Van de Kamp

Major funding provided by entertainment
industry, environmental groups, and the
Environmental Protection Initiative

Printed on recycled paper

[handwritten: FYI
Aug 23 9 AM '90]

August 22, 1990

TO:     Fair Political Practices Commission
        Post Office Box 807
        Sacramento CA 95804

FROM:   Bob Mulholland          926 J Street, #1400
                               Sacramento CA 95814
RE:     Proposition 105.        916-447-8950  PHONE

I have a question about the following scenario for two
recipient committees.

The Big Green (Proposition 128) campaign sets up two
committees:

The first committee we'll call "Committee M".  The other
committee we'll refer to as "Committee B".

Both committees will be controlled by the same campaign
firm, the same sponsors, have the same campaign address,
and will be working in concert.

The "M" committee will be an intermediary for funds for
the Big Green campaign.

The "B" committee will be used to buy advertising.  The
two committees have only one purpose - the Big Green
initiative.  That's their stated purpose, as defined in
their Statements of Organization (Forms 410), filed with
the Secretary of State.  All contributions are given for
that purpose.

In determining the 105 criteria for the "B" committee,
would we have to consider the funds given to the "M"
committee.

Additionally, a high percentage of the contributions to
the "M" committee are from out of state, and are then
deposited in the "M" committee bank account in
California.

When committee "M" writes out a check to committee "B",
can we then consider the funds "from" California?


Page 2

Could we possibly avoid the 105 disclosure requirements
with the "B" committee by claiming the money that it got
from the "M" committee is "neutral" money?

Given that the election is November 6, please advise us
as soon as possible.

Sincerely,

Bob Mulholland
Campaign Manager

I see a mostly blank or very faded document. There appear to be some very faint horizontal lines or marks across the page, but no clearly legible text is visible in this image. The document appears to be aged or heavily faded, with only the paper texture and some subtle markings discernible.

[illegible]
